docs.title: Welcome to Fedora Data Space Documentation
docs.text: The FEDORA Mobility Data Space supports the project’s federated approach to advanced traffic and network management. It provides a shared catalog where project partners and stakeholders can discover, publish, access, and reuse datasets for multi-modal mobility research, demonstrations, optimization services, and simulation workflows.

docs.title: Quick Navigation
docs.text:
• Fedora Overview — Understand the project scope and objectives
  • Harvesting Workflow — Automating the harvesting process.
  • AI Assistant — Explore available dataset using natural langage.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: View the latest changes in the Changelog.

docs.title: Added
docs.text: • Initial documentation site setup

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: The AI Assistant is a core component of the data space that provides intelligent analysis, pattern detection, and natural language interaction with your data.

docs.title: Data Search
docs.text:
• Optimized Search — Quickly find relevant datasets with fast keyword-based search.
  • Natural Langage Exploration — Enable users to explore semantically using natural langage.

docs.title: 1. Introduction
docs.text: MobilityDCAT-AP

docs.title: How to Harvest
docs.text: Harvesting can be configured either manually through the CKAN graphical interface or automatically using the fedora-harvester tool. This guide covers the automatic approach, which offers greater efficiency and reproducibility.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before proceeding, ensure that you have completed the Installation steps and have access to the Data Catalogue platform (based on CKAN).

The following image shows the home page of the Data Catalogue:

docs.title: 1. Obtain an API Token
docs.text:
An API token is required to authorise remote calls to the Data Catalogue.

To generate a token, navigate to Admin → API Token, as illustrated below.

docs.title: 2. Configure fedora-harvester
docs.text:
Once the installation is complete, verify that fedora-harvester is installed and functioning correctly.

Run the following command:

fedora-harvester --help

You should see the output below, which confirms that the tool is operational but not yet configured:

Complete the initial configuration by running the command provided in the output.

Once configured, run fedora-harvester --help again. The full list of available options should now be displayed:

docs.title: 3. Prepare the CSV Source File
docs.text:
All required information for defining organisations and data sources must be provided in a CSV file.

The example above contains two sources to harvest.

As shown in the fedora-harvester --help output, you can harvest either all sources or a specific row.

docs.title: Harvesting a Specific Row
docs.text:
To harvest a single row from the CSV file, run:

fedora-harvester -n <number_row> harvest <csv_file_path>

docs.title: Harvesting All Rows
docs.text:
To harvest all rows in the CSV file, run:

fedora-harvester harvest <csv_file_path>

docs.title: Result
docs.text: Upon successful completion, all information defined in the CSV source file will be reflected in the Data Catalogue. The following image shows the expected result:

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text: This guide has walk you through the complete harvesting workflow using fedora-harvester — from obtaining an API token and configuring the tool to executing harvest jobs. By leveraging this automated approach, you can efficiently manage and update your Data Catalogue with minimal manual intervention.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Before setting up harvesting, you need to install three key components:

  1. ckanext-harvest – the core harvesting framework for CKAN
  2. ckanext-dcat – extension for handling DCAT datasets and vocabularies
  3. A fedora-harvesting automation tool – developed to streamline and automate the harvesting process from csv file.

This guide assumes you already have a running CKAN instance.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• A working CKAN installation (version 2.9 or later recommended)
  • Python 3.7+
  • pip and virtualenv (if using virtual environments)
  • Git

docs.title: Installing ckanext-harvest
docs.text:
Provides the core harvesting framework for fetching metadata from remote sources using protocols like OAI-PMH, CSW, and SPARQL.

# Navigate to your CKAN virtual environment
cd /usr/lib/ckan/default
source bin/activate

# Install from PyPI
pip install -e git+https://github.com/ckan/ckanext-harvest.git#egg=ckanext-harvest

# Alternatively, install the latest release
pip install ckanext-harvest

Add harvest to the ckan.plugins line in your ckan.ini configuration file:

ckan.plugins = ... harvest

docs.title: Installing ckanext-dcat
docs.text:
Adds support for DCAT vocabulary to expose and harvest datasets in RDF and JSON-LD formats.

# In the same virtual environment
pip install -e git+https://github.com/ckan/ckanext-dcat.git#egg=ckanext-dcat

# Or install via PyPI
pip install ckanext-dcat

Add dcat to the ckan.plugins line in ckan.ini:

ckan.plugins = ... harvest dcat

docs.title: Installing the Fedora-Harvester Automation Tool
docs.text:
This tool helps us to automate CKAN harvest jobs from a CSV definition file via the ckanext-harvest API.

Run the following command : 

# Clone the custom tool repository
git clone https://gitlab.akka.eu/fedora/fedora-harvester.git
cd ckan-harvest-automation

# Install dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Install the extension
pip install .

docs.title: Final Configuration
docs.text:
After installing all three extensions, restart CKAN:

# If using supervisor
sudo supervisorctl restart ckan

Verify the installation:

ckan -c /etc/ckan/default/ckan.ini sysadmin add admin
ckan -c /etc/ckan/default/ckan.ini harvester init

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Ensure all extensions are in the ckan.plugins line in the correct order.
  • Check the CKAN error log for missing dependencies.
  • Verify the database tables were created by the harvest init command.

For more details, refer to the official documentation: - ckanext-harvest - ckanext-dcat

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Metadata harvesting is the process of collecting and aggregating dataset metadata from distributed sources into a centralized catalogue. In the context of CKAN, harvesting enables the automatic retrieval of metadata records using standardised machine-readable formats such as RDF and JSON-LD.

This capability is essential for maintaining an up-to-date and comprehensive data catalogue, particularly when source data is managed across multiple organisations or platforms.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
The harvesting framework relies on three key components:

  1. ckanext-harvest — The core harvesting extension for CKAN. It provides the infrastructure for defining, scheduling, and executing harvest jobs against remote data sources.

  2. ckanext-dcat — An extension that adds support for the DCAT vocabulary, enabling datasets to be exposed and harvested in RDF and JSON-LD formats.

  3. fedora-harvester — A custom automation tool developed to streamline the harvesting process. It allows harvest jobs to be configured and executed directly from a CSV definition file, eliminating the need for manual setup through the CKAN interface.

docs.title: What Sets Fedora Data Space Apart
docs.text: FDR introduces several innovative approaches to data management and analysis that distinguish it from traditional solutions.

docs.title: Core Mission
docs.text:
Fedora Data Space aims to:

  1. Simplify data management — Reduce the complexity of handling diverse data sources
  2. Ehance data exploration - Accelerate data discovery and generate insights with ease.
  3. Improve data interoperability - Ensure seamless data sharing and integration across applications and environments.

docs.title: Target Users
docs.text:
• Data Scientist/Engineer managing data collection.
  • Partners looking for specific dataset.
